stdout:
I’m switching to repository-native tools and sub-agents since direct shell execution is blocked here. Next I’m parallelizing validation and diff inspection, then I’ll spot-check the changed files for exact citations.

I’ve confirmed the core output-path changes. Now I’m checking for gaps the prior reviews called out: remaining JSON success paths without messages, and whether the earlier blockers are still actually present.

Validation is mostly green now: `jit-validate`, `fmt`, `clippy`, and MCP tests all pass. I’m checking whether the remaining failure is only the two unrelated Rust tests or whether the implementation itself still misses any of the issue’s explicit acceptance criteria.

I’ve got the remaining blocker narrowed down. The earlier `gate check-all` and docs issues are fixed, and validation is green except for two Rust tests that still fail, which is enough to fail the review under this repo’s rules.

## Acceptance criteria

Most of the implementation criteria are met.

`JsonOutput::with_message()` exists and is documented at `crates/jit/src/output.rs:128-159`, with unit coverage including the array-payload edge case at `crates/jit/src/output.rs:795-830`.

Representative CLI JSON paths now carry a human-readable message, e.g. `issue create` at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:274-278`, `gate check-all` at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1288-1306`, `status` at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3364-3370`, and `claim status/list` at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:4073-4081` and `4153-4161`.

MCP summary/compaction is generic: `generateUserSummary()` only reads `result.message` at `mcp-server/index.js:75-82`, and `compactForAssistant()` has no command-name branches at `mcp-server/index.js:92-129`. `mcp-server/index.js` is now 347 LOC.

`dep add` / `dep rm` direction help is clear at `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:483-514`.

Blocking failure: the issue explicitly requires `cargo test` to pass, but it does not.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No blocking boundary violation found in the reviewed change set.

Output shaping stays in `crates/jit/src/output.rs:128-159` and `crates/jit/src/output_macros.rs:26-96`.

Command orchestration stays in `crates/jit/src/main.rs`, for example `gate check-all` at `1288-1306`.

CLI help text changes are isolated to `crates/jit/src/cli.rs:483-514`.

MCP formatting logic is centralized in `mcp-server/index.js:75-129` rather than spreading command-specific logic further.

## Functional paradigm

The changed code is consistent with project style.

Rust paths use iterator combinators, e.g. passed-gate counting in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1291-1294`.

The MCP server uses generic transformations instead of command-specific branching at `mcp-server/index.js:92-129`.

No blocking functional-style issue found.

## Correctness and invariants

The earlier `gate check-all` message-loss bug is fixed: it now emits an object payload plus top-level message at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1288-1306`.

I found no new violations around atomic writes, event logging, assignee format, label format, or PID safety in the touched code.

Repository correctness still fails because the required test suite is red.

## Safety

No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed areas, and nothing here appears to weaken the workspace’s `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` posture.

## Testing

This is the blocking area.

Verified checks:
- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passed.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` passed.
- `cd mcp-server && npm test` passed (52 tests).
- `cargo test --workspace` failed: 659 passed, 2 failed.

The failing assertions are:
- `crates/jit/src/commands/gate_check.rs:1122-1125`
- `crates/jit/src/gate_execution.rs:570-581`

Both expect stdout to contain `CTX=unset` when `pass_context: false`. Per this review’s rules, even pre-existing failures block approval.

## Error handling

No new broad catches or silent success-shaped fallbacks were introduced in the reviewed diff.

The remaining failure is not error-handling debt in this change; it is the unresolved red test suite above.

## CLI contract

The reviewed commands continue to support `--json`.

The MCP contract now correctly prefers CLI-authored messages via `result.message` at `mcp-server/index.js:75-82`.

## Documentation

The prior docs gap is fixed: `JsonOutput::with_message()` now includes a `# Examples` section at `crates/jit/src/output.rs:134-144`.

## Prior review feedback

The most recent substantive review reported three blockers. Two are addressed:
- The stale JIT document reference is gone; `.jit/issues/eb5b6ffe-f47c-4405-97c9-59b1b3b05bf2.json:44-49` now shows `documents: []`.
- The missing `with_message()` example docs are fixed at `crates/jit/src/output.rs:134-144`.

One remains unresolved:
- `cargo test` still fails at `crates/jit/src/commands/gate_check.rs:1122-1125` and `crates/jit/src/gate_execution.rs:570-581`.

## JIT issue management

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there is no prerequisite-work blocker.

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passes; only non-blocking label warnings remain.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
